methods.method_name: Spectral Analysis of Proxy and Model Data
methods.description: This method involves the pre-processing and spectral analysis of proxy and model data to study multi-centennial climate variability. It includes filtering high-frequency noise, interpolating data, and performing spectral analysis using the REDFIT program.

steps.description: Remove high-frequency noise using a low-pass Butterworth filter in both the proxies and model data.
steps.searchable_summary: apply low-pass Butterworth filter to remove high-frequency noise

steps.description: Interpolate proxy data with an Akima interpolation to a 10-year, evenly spaced resolution.
steps.searchable_summary: interpolate proxy data using Akima method to 10-year resolution

steps.description: Apply Butterworth filter to filter out oscillations with frequencies higher than 20 years for proxy data and 60 years for model data.
steps.searchable_summary: filter oscillations above 20 years for proxy and 60 years for model data

steps.description: Perform spectral analysis using the REDFIT program adapted to the R package, including a significance test against red noise at the 95% significance level.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct spectral analysis with REDFIT and test against red noise

steps.description: Apply kernel density distributions to illustrate the results in different proxy and model groups.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize results using kernel density distributions

methods.description: This method describes the setup and analysis of transient model simulations to study Holocene climate variability, focusing on temperature data and comparing different models and forcing scenarios.

steps.description: Collect model data from nine different models with their experimental setup for each simulation.
steps.searchable_summary: gather model data and experimental setups from nine models

steps.description: Use surface temperature (ts) for some models and 2 m near surface air temperature (tas) for others to make model data more comparable.
steps.searchable_summary: standardize temperature data using ts and tas

steps.description: Analyze annual mean data from model output for global mean temperature (GMT) and six latitude bands.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze GMT and latitude bands from model output

steps.description: Compare differentiated forcing simulations and analyze their impact on multi-centennial variability.
steps.searchable_summary: compare and analyze differentiated forcing simulations
